changesProposed.name: Humanoid

verified.name: Humanoid
verified.description: AI-driven industrial humanoid robot company developing HMND 01 Alpha Wheeled for warehouse, logistics, and manufacturing applications. The robot features dual arms, proprietary KinetIQ AI framework, and operates autonomously with 15kg payload capacity.
verified.notes: Database entry claimed $50M founder-led capital and Seed stage; sources verify $152M Series A at $1.35B valuation instead. Wikipedia source [2] is about the term 'humanoid' generally, not the company, so not used. Source [1] lists Artem Sokolov in team archive but does not explicitly identify him as founder/co-founder; however combined with sources [3,5] context, he appears to be founder. No founding year found in sources.

changesProposed.name: Science Corporation

verified.name: Science Corporation
verified.description: Clinical-stage medical technology company developing brain-computer interfaces and vision restoration technology, including the PRIMA retinal implant for treating age-related macular degeneration.
verified.notes: Most recent funding round is Series C ($230M closed March 5, 2026 per source 0). Database entry listed Series B with $200M+, which is superseded by Series C figure. Founded year not explicitly stated in sources. Max Hodak is named as CEO and founder in source 1 leadership section. Investor names from database entry could not be verified in provided sources.

changesProposed.name: SpaceX

verified.name: SpaceX
verified.description: American spaceflight, telecommunications, and artificial intelligence company that conducts more orbital launches annually than any other launch provider and operates Starlink, a communications satellite constellation.
verified.notes: Wikipedia source indicates IPO occurred on June 12, 2026, raising $86B. Database entry claims 'preparing for IPO' at $1.5T valuation, but sources confirm IPO already occurred and raised $86B, not $1.65T valuation. Valuation field left null as no current valuation figure found in sources.

changesProposed.name: Ursa Major Technologies

verified.name: Ursa Major Technologies
verified.description: American aerospace company that produces rocket engines and propulsion systems for space launch, hypersonics, and defense applications. The company delivers liquid and solid rocket engines using additive manufacturing and modular design approaches.
verified.notes: Database entry lists Series E stage and $400M+ raised, but sources only verify Series C with $85M in December 2021. No evidence of later funding rounds in provided sources. Most recent specific funding information from Wikipedia is December 2021 Series C led by BlackRock.
